qa_pairs.answer: The address for the Cedar cloud is cedar.cloud.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar cloud is located at Simon Fraser University.

qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar cloud has a total of 7,680 GB of RAM.

qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar cloud has 500 TB of persistent Ceph storage.

qa_pairs.answer: The 28-node configuration of the Cedar cloud uses 2 x Intel Xeon E5-2683 v4 CPUs per node.

qa_pairs.answer: The 28-node type in the Cedar cloud uses 1 x 10GbE interconnect.

qa_pairs.question: What OpenStack platform version does Arbutus cloud use as of February 23, 2026?
qa_pairs.answer: As of February 23, 2026, the Arbutus cloud uses the Ussuri OpenStack platform version.

qa_pairs.question: What OpenStack platform version is used for Cedar cloud as of February 23, 2026?
qa_pairs.answer: As of February 23, 2026, the Cedar cloud uses the Train OpenStack platform version.

qa_pairs.question: What OpenStack platform version is used for Nibi cloud as of February 23, 2026?
qa_pairs.answer: As of February 23, 2026, the Nibi cloud uses the Flamingo OpenStack platform version.

qa_pairs.question: What OpenStack platform version is used for Béluga cloud as of February 23, 2026?
qa_pairs.answer: As of February 23, 2026, the Béluga cloud uses the Victoria OpenStack platform version.

qa_pairs.question: What is the general date for the listed Alliance cloud OpenStack platform versions?
qa_pairs.answer: The listed Alliance cloud OpenStack platform versions are as of February 23, 2026.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find a list of all OpenStack versions?
qa_pairs.answer: A list of all OpenStack versions can be found on the OpenStack releases website.

qa_pairs.question: Who provides images on the Alliance Clouds?
qa_pairs.answer: Images on the Alliance Clouds are provided by Alliance staff.

qa_pairs.question: What common Linux distributions are available as images on the Alliance Clouds?
qa_pairs.answer: Common Linux distributions available as images on the Alliance Clouds include Alma, Debian, Fedora, Rocky, and Ubuntu.

qa_pairs.question: How often are new images for Linux distributions added?
qa_pairs.answer: New images for Linux distributions are added periodically as new releases and updates become available.

qa_pairs.question: What is recommended for users when Linux distribution releases reach their end of life (EOL)?
qa_pairs.answer: Users are encouraged to migrate systems and platforms to newer releases to continue receiving patches and security updates when Linux distribution releases reach their end of life (EOL).

qa_pairs.question: What happens to older images for Linux distributions that are past their end of life (EOL)?
qa_pairs.answer: Older images for Linux distributions past their EOL will be removed, meaning they will no longer be available when creating new VMs.

qa_pairs.question: Does the removal of EOL images prevent users from running existing VMs with EOL Linux distributions?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the removal of EOL images does not prevent users from continuing to run an existing VM with an EOL Linux distribution, although it is not recommended.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find more details about working with images?
qa_pairs.answer: More details about working with images can be found on the 'Working with images' page.
